subject: Re: How to skip some views in warp's tomogram reconstruction?
body:
Dear Alister,
Hi, thank you for your reply, I think that should solve my question, thank 
you very much!
Best,
Linhua Tai

On Monday, March 23, 2020 at 10:53:49 PM UTC+8, Alister Burt wrote:
>
> Hi Linhua,
>
> When you reconstruct subtomograms there’s an option to limit to the first 
> N tilts by dose, I don’t think it’s available for full tomograms but 
> shouldn’t be necessary!
>
> Best,
>
> Alister
>
> On 23 Mar 2020, at 15:06, Linhua Tai <888...@gmail.com <javascript:>> 
> wrote:
>
> ﻿
> Dear all,
> Hi, another silly question, how can I skip some views in tomogram 
> reconstruction in warp?
> The problem for me is, I collected data as -60 to +60 in 3 degree step, 
> resulted in 41 views each tilt series, as I use dose symmetric scheme, my 
> sample starts bubbling at high tilts. For picking particles and initial 
> refinement I use full views for each tilt series, but for final refinement 
> I would like to use views between -45 to +45 degrees. As the .xf file and 
> taSolution.log I have is correspond to full 41 views, I'm curious if 
> there's a way for me to skip views as -60 to -45 & +45 to +60 degrees to 
> generate the subvolumes for final refinement? Also, if there is a way to do 
> so, does that way the same as I should do for the subsequent M refinement?
> Thank you very much in advance!
> Yours sincerely,
> Linhua Tai
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
> "Warp" group.

> .
>
>
